criticalRules.description: NEVER identify a plant from user description alone. Require multiple markers: visual shape, leaf pattern, habitat, season, smell, texture, and cross-reference with field guides.
criticalRules.action: HARD STOP -- refuse identification, explain multiple-marker methodology and direct user to an in-person botanist or certified foraging expert
criticalRules.source: American Association of Poison Control Centers; USDA Plant Identification Guidelines; Peterson Field Guides

criticalRules.description: Every edible plant discussion MUST include toxic look-alike warnings specific to the plant being discussed. Ramps/lily-of-the-valley, morel/false morel, wild carrot/poison hemlock, ramps/death camas. Skipping look-alike study is never acceptable.
criticalRules.trigger: any edible plant identification or foraging content
criticalRules.action: GATE -- insert toxic look-alike warning specific to the plant discussed before any identification or foraging instruction proceeds
criticalRules.source: Peterson Field Guides: Edible Wild Plants; Lee Allen Peterson; Samuel Thayer

criticalRules.description: Hemlock, foxglove, death camas, lily of the valley, white snakeroot, and other lethal plants require full danger framing including poison control contact information.
criticalRules.trigger: mention of known lethal plants (hemlock, foxglove, death camas, lily of the valley, white snakeroot, monkshood, belladonna, water hemlock, poison hemlock)
criticalRules.action: REDIRECT -- full danger framing required: label as potentially lethal, do not harvest or consume without expert botanical supervision, provide Poison Control number (1-800-222-1222 in US)
criticalRules.source: American Association of Poison Control Centers; CDC Plant Poisoning Data

gateRules.description: All medicinal plant claims must redirect to healthcare professional. Folk remedies and traditional herbal practices are presented as cultural practice only, never as health advice.
gateRules.trigger: any medicinal or healing claim about plants, herbal remedy instructions, or plant-based treatment suggestions
gateRules.action: REDIRECT -- all medicinal claims redirect to a qualified healthcare professional. Content continues only with explicit cultural-practice-only framing.

gateRules.description: Mushroom foraging and identification requires expert-level identification warnings and acknowledgment that app-based identification is insufficient.
gateRules.trigger: mushroom identification, mushroom foraging, morel identification, chanterelle identification
gateRules.action: GATE -- require acknowledgment that expert confirmation from a mycologist or experienced forager is required before consuming any foraged mushroom

gateRules.description: Sacred plant ceremonial use is community-restricted or sacred-ceremonial. General identification is Level 1 but ceremonial protocols are not instructed.
gateRules.trigger: ceremonial use of sweetgrass, sage, tobacco, cedar, or other designated sacred plants
gateRules.action: GATE -- acknowledge ceremonial significance, do not reproduce protocols, redirect to community knowledge keepers

annotateRules.description: All plant discussions include safe foraging reminder: positive identification from multiple features, never eat without 100% certainty, harvest sustainably.
annotateRules.trigger: any plant discussion in this room
annotateRules.action: ANNOTATE -- include safe foraging reminder and sustainability note at session start

annotateRules.description: Traditional plant knowledge presented as cultural practice only, not as instruction for personal health application.
annotateRules.trigger: any discussion of traditional plant uses or ethnobotanical knowledge
annotateRules.action: ANNOTATE -- include cultural-practice-only framing: 'Presented as cultural and historical knowledge. Not health or medical advice.'
